spec_match.rationale: Each component panel sets anchor-scope: --menu-anchor; each internal trigger declares anchor-name: --menu-anchor; popover is positioned via position-anchor. Canonical 'component encapsulation' pattern.

interactive.rationale: Two tab buttons swap scope-on vs scope-none on both components; menu triggers toggle popovers. Real interactivity.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises 'two instances of the same component each anchor a popover internally without leaking names'; page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Title says 'inheritance' but the demo doesn't actually show INHERITANCE — it shows applying anchor-scope directly to each component root. True inheritance would be setting anchor-scope on an ancestor and watching descendant anchors all confined. Slug name and content mismatch.

references.rationale: See also links to chromestatus.com/feature/6243475673317376 — WRONG feature ID. This concept is part of feature 5094192052436992. Critical rule #3 regression.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout. Menu trigger / popover use design system tokens consistently.

openQuestions.title: Fix the wrong chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: See also points at chromestatus.com/feature/6243475673317376 but this concept is part of feature 5094192052436992. The slug-recovery script would relocate this folder under the wrong feature. Update the URL.

openQuestions.title: Show actual inheritance, not just direct application
openQuestions.detail: Slug `scope-inheritance` suggests a demo of anchor-scope on an ANCESTOR affecting descendant anchor lookups. Right now scope is applied directly on the component root. Either rename the concept to `component-encapsulation` (better matches what it does), or wrap the components in a parent that holds the scope.

summary: Strong demonstration of the encapsulation use case, but mislabelled as 'inheritance' and ships the wrong chromestatus feature ID — both regressions worth fixing immediately.
